description: User adds a custom column, removes it, adds another, removes it. Each change = new iteration. All 4 iterations trackable in UI with correct columns. Tests regex vs AI detection, configurable columns, and historical viewing.

steps.description: User asks to add employee size classification — MCP detects this as regex, NOT AI
steps.user_prompt: Add a column that classifies companies by size: LARGE if 100+ employees, MEDIUM if 20-99, SMALL if under 20
user_prompt_variants: I want a size_segment column: large = 100+, medium = 20-99, small = <20 employees
Classify companies by headcount into SMALL, MEDIUM, LARGE
steps.critical_requirement: MCP MUST detect this as a regex/algorithmic step. Employee count comparison does NOT need GPT. Execution should be instant (no API calls).

steps.description: Verify the pipeline table now shows the size_segment column

user_prompt_variants: I don't need size_segment anymore, remove it
steps.critical_requirement: Column is removed from current view. But iteration 1 STILL shows it (historical). Iteration 2 shows no custom columns.

steps.description: User switches to iteration 1 in the selector — size_segment should reappear
User asks to add a classification that REQUIRES AI — business model detection
steps.user_prompt: Add a column that classifies whether each company is a fashion BRAND (designs own clothes) or a MANUFACTURER (makes clothes for others) based on their website
user_prompt_variants: I want to know which companies are brands vs manufacturers — analyze their websites
Add business_model column: BRAND if they sell own brand, MANUFACTURER if they produce for others
Classify brand vs manufacturer from website content
steps.critical_requirement: This REQUIRES AI because it needs to understand website content. MCP should build a detailed classification prompt (like the Drive JSON examples) and show it for approval.

steps.user_prompt: Actually remove business_model, I don't need it

steps.description: ALL 4 iterations must be selectable and show correct columns
steps.critical_requirement: The iteration dropdown in the top-left of the pipeline page must show all 4 iterations. Switching between them shows/hides the appropriate custom columns. Historical data is preserved.

screenshots_needed.check: Pipeline table shows size_segment column with SMALL/MEDIUM/LARGE values
Pipeline table has NO size_segment column
Pipeline table shows business_model column with BRAND/MANUFACTURER values
Pipeline table has NO business_model column
Show/hide toggles for all columns including custom ones

db_checks: pipeline_iterations has 4 records for Result project
rules_verified: Regex step (size) executed instantly without API calls
AI step (business_model) required OpenAI key and showed prompt for approval
Essential columns (domain, name, is_target) CANNOT be removed
Each add/remove creates new iteration number
All iterations selectable in UI with correct column visibility
